constraints: Stage 1 and Stage 2 are divided into bounded evidence-producing milestones
Each milestone receives a predeclared effort or calendar budget before implementation
Reaching a cap pauses work for written evidence overrun scope extension pivot or stop review
Extensions require a specific evidence or dependency reason a revised cap and unchanged acceptance evidence
Polish generalization sunk-cost pressure and routine overtime do not silently move the gate
Preserve core mechanics representative content readability controls diagnostics valid testing accessibility reliability and gate-relevant save or performance work
Cut additional weapons enemies rooms variants cosmetic polish secondary progression mature economy optional tools generalized architecture and premature post-MVP preparation first
Every cut records its reason and re-entry condition
Deferred scope cannot silently return during the same milestone
Validation accessibility diagnostics and reliability are not traded away to preserve visible feature count
Record the product question evidence outcomes scope method population and approvers before work begins
The core question cannot be softened after implementation or evidence collection starts
Clarifications are allowed only before affected evidence is collected while material changes create a new version or test round
Previous results remain attached to their original rules
Technical failures remain distinct from fun evidence and threshold changes require measurement-validity justification

unselected_question.handoff: Planning must define a small debt register and explicit Stage 2 exit conditions without treating the presented recommendation as an accepted user decision.

open_questions_added: Exact milestone budgets and formal test thresholds
Recovered D-001 through D-039 remain excluded unless explicitly re-verified

next_action.instruction: Review and merge the requirements/bootstrap package. After merge, begin a fresh Planning Agent context from the merged project_intake.json and REQUIREMENTS.md.
